content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 9, 1989


Judge Winton McKibben
Chair, Grand Jury Committee
Alameda County Superior Court
1225 Fallon Street
Oakland, California  94612

                                        Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                             Our File No. I-89-217

Dear Judge McKibben:

     You have forwarded for our review a copy of a proposed
statement of economic interests for Alameda County Grand Jurors.
Your letter indicates that the proposed statement is submitted in
accordance with our letter of March 3, 1987, involving financial
disclosure by grand jurors.  Please note that our letter requested
that counties submit copies of the conflict-of-interest codes
which counties prepare describing the disclosure obligations of
grand jurors.  We would appreciate receiving a copy of that code.

     Please also note that the statement of economic interests
must be filed by grand jurors on the Commission's Form 730.
(Walenta Advice Letter, No. A-81-144; Cassady Advice Letter, No.
A-88-106; copies enclosed.)

     If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5901.

                                        Sincerely

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: John G. McLean
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JGM:aa

Enclosures



428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

SUPERIOR COURT
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
COUNTY OF ALAMEDA

CHAMBERS OF
WINTON McKIBBEN
JUDGE

COURTHOUSE
1225 FALLON STREET
OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94612

April 3, 1989

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J. Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA.  95804-0807

Enclosed, please find a proposed statement of economic
interests for Alameda County Grand Jurors.  This proposed
statement contains the four specific areas outlined in a
memorandum to "interested parties" from your office ad
March 3, 1987.  This proposed statement is being forwarded
to your office in reference to a letter from
Diane M. Griffiths, General Counsel, by John G. McLean to
the Superior Court Presiding Judge of Amador County, which
requested a copy of any proposed statement be sent to your
agency.

If you have comments on this proposed statement, please
forward them to me at your earliest convenience.  Thank you
for your consideration.

Very truly yours,

Judge Winton McKibben
Chair, Grand Jury Committee

WM:ck

cc:  Michael E. Ballachey, Presiding Judge
Douglas Hickling, County Counsel
Stacy Walthall, Legal Advisor to
Alameda County Grand Jury
Joanne Lederman, Executive Officer
Members Grand Jury Committee


rolet.049

ALAMEDA COUNTY GRAND JURY

STATEMENT OF ECONOMIC INTERESTS


1.    List below any interests you have in real property in
      Alameda County:

      a.  _____________________________________________

      b.  _____________________________________________

      c.  _____________________________________________

      (attach additional sheets as necessary)

2.    List below any investments and business positions held
      by you in business entities which have done business
      with the County of Alameda in the previous two years:

      a.  _____________________________________________

      b.  _____________________________________________

      c.  _____________________________________________

      (attached additional sheets as necessary)

3.    List below any income you have received from business
      entities which have done business with the County of
      Alameda in the previous two years:

      a.  _____________________________________________

      b.  _____________________________________________

      c.  _____________________________________________

4.    List below any income you receive from individuals
     who are County of Alameda employees:

     a.    _____________________________________________

     b.    _____________________________________________

     c.    _____________________________________________

     (attach additional sheets as necessary)

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 14, 1989


Honorable Winton McKibben
Judge of the Superior Court
County of Alameda
Chair, Grand Jury Committee
Courthouse
1225 Fallon Street
Oakland, CA  94612

Re:  Letter No. 89-217

Dear Judge McKibben:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on April 12, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

sections.conclusion: Please note that the statement of economic interests must be filed by grand jurors on the Commission's Form 730. We would appreciate receiving a copy of the conflict-of-interest code which counties prepare describing the disclosure obligations of grand jurors.
sections.question_synthetic: Judge McKibben submitted a proposed statement of economic interests form for Alameda County Grand Jurors and sought FPPC review and approval regarding compliance with financial disclosure obligations.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Grand jurors must file their statements of economic interests using the Commission's Form 730, and the FPPC requested that Alameda County submit a copy of its conflict-of-interest code describing grand jury disclosure obligations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple letters: incoming request from Judge McKibben (April 3, 1989), FPPC acknowledgment letter (April 14, 1989), and FPPC substantive response (June 9, 1989). The June 9 letter is the primary FPPC advice response. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers present; content extracted from letter body.

QUESTION: Judge McKibben submitted a proposed statement of economic interests form for Alameda County Grand Jurors and sought FPPC review and approval regarding compliance with financial disclosure obligations.

CONCLUSION: Please note that the statement of economic interests must be filed by grand jurors on the Commission's Form 730. We would appreciate receiving a copy of the conflict-of-interest code which counties prepare describing the disclosure obligations of grand jurors.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to Alameda County's submission of a proposed economic interests statement for grand jurors, advising that grand jurors must use Form 730 and requesting submission of the county's conflict-of-interest code.
